title: RavenClaude comfort posture
description: Categorical permission-autonomy scale for Claude Code. Edited via this plugin's dashboard.html Settings tab; saved to .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml in the consumer's project. The /set-posture skill (proposal 002 §5) translates this YAML into .claude/settings.json permission rules.

cautious._description: Maximum oversight. Every non-trivial action asks first. Recommended starting point per Koch et al.'s 'OK blindness' finding.

default._description: Balanced. Reads freely; asks before edits, network writes, package installs, and arbitrary code execution.

productive._description: Maximum velocity. Edits and local mutations flow without asking. Network writes, package installs, arbitrary code exec, and global writes still ask.

global_default.title: Global default
global_default.description: Used for any category not explicitly set below. Apply a preset to set this and all categories at once.

categories.title: Per-category overrides
categories.description: Each category's autonomy level. Cautious = always ask. Default = ask in ambiguous cases. Productive = act without asking.

file_read_project.title: Read files in this project
file_read_project.description: ls, cat, grep, reading source files inside the current project directory.

file_edit_project.title: Edit files in this project
file_edit_project.description: Write, Edit, MultiEdit on files inside the current project. Set lower if you want to review every change.

file_read_global.title: Read files outside this project
file_read_global.description: Reading ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, ~/.config, /etc, etc. Almost always wants to be cautious.

file_edit_global.title: Edit files outside this project
file_edit_global.description: Writing to your home directory or system paths. Almost always wants to be cautious.

shell_readonly.title: Read-only shell commands
shell_readonly.description: ls, cat, git status, grep, find. The cheap stuff. Productive means "never ask."

shell_local_mutate.title: Local mutations
shell_local_mutate.description: mkdir, mv, cp, git commit. Changes your filesystem but doesn't talk to a server.

shell_remote_mutate.title: Remote mutations
shell_remote_mutate.description: git push, gh pr create, gh pr comment. Anything that publishes to a shared system.

shell_code_exec.title: Arbitrary code execution
shell_code_exec.description: python -c, node -e, bash -c, eval. Recommend cautious — these are general-purpose.

shell_package_install.title: Package installs
shell_package_install.description: npm install, pip install, apt install, brew install. Recommend cautious — these can run install scripts.

network_read.title: Web reads
network_read.description: WebFetch on known documentation domains. Productive means trusted domains never ask.

network_write.title: Web writes
network_write.description: POST/PUT/DELETE to web endpoints. Almost always wants to be cautious.

mcp_tools.title: MCP server tools
mcp_tools.description: Tools provided by connected MCP servers. Per-server trust lives in your Claude Code config; this is the global default.
